I had already been in court for over 3 years, had a previous GAL and multiple pretrial hearings. However, due to unforeseen circumstances, my daughter's therapist was no longer available and I had a switch of judges one month before my case would have been finalized. My ex took advantage of the change in judge, requested a new GAL, lied about everything, and made constant accusations through motions. He put me in a position where my daughter was temporarily taken from me.

This is where Tom came in. My former attorney told me, "He's a good guy" and worked to transfer all documentation to him. I had heard his name and his brother's name constantly being assigned as a GAL in other cases at court. When he took over my case, he came in ready to battle. His expertise as a GAL and highly respected reputation in court, made my ex know he was not playing their games. He was very thorough and advised me on the best course of action to take. He got my daughter back to me right away.

In the middle of all of this, I was diagnosed with cancer and my ex wasted no time to use this against me. All I can say is that Tom protected me, but made sure he did what was best for my daughter. I know this was the GAL in him. He never made needless motions or meetings to extract extra money as I have heard so many other attorneys do. Every action he took was with a purpose and to get an agreement in place. No matter what angle my ex took with his legal team, Tom was ready.

Unfortunately, I did have to go to trial. But every move, every single question Tom made was done with a purpose. My ex had 2 attorneys, who were both also GALs, representing him. I had Tom (GAL) and there was the court assigned GAL to my case. At trial, the court had 4 GALS in the room, and the best won, which was Tom of course. He blew the others out of the water. My sister who has worked with multiple law firms in downtown Chicago was extremely impressed by him.

My ex even accused me of being "terminal" to DCFS, in order to get custody of my daughter. It was a clown show with him and Tom made sure it was evident in court. If it wasn't for Tom, I sincerely doubt I would have gotten my daughter back. As a single mother of 2 girls, Tom came in as my savior, and this is not an exaggeration. At court, the majority of time I went alone, but having Tom as my representation, I breathed a sigh of relief and gained strength and courage knowing that he was by my side.

I am forever grateful for Tom for all that he did. I would highly recommend him (and constantly do), especially in high conflict cases.

When the phone rings at 3 a.m. you know it's not going to be good news—but you take the call anyway. In this instance the call was from a hospital in Hawaii telling me my mother had just passed away while on a cruise. Needless to say, when I landed in Hawaii 8 hours later, I was still in a state of shock and unsure of what to do next. The only thing I could think to do was call the law firm listed on my mother's will—Perkins & Zayed.

It turns out that this was the best decision I could have made. On my first call I was introduced to Carrie, a paralegal of the highest caliber who expertly fielded all my questions and walked me through the process, stem to stern. Her candor and unflappable personality were an absolute solace in my hour of need. She truly went above and beyond—someone you definitely want in your corner. She. Is. Great.

When it came time to sell my mom's house, I was full of questions and reached out to P&Z again. This time I was introduced to Jennifer Eseed, a super-sharp attorney who handles real estate for the firm. She immediately put my mind at rest, answering all of my questions with an ebullient attitude and a reserve of patience I could only describe as Saint-like. While I'm sure there are plenty of good attorneys out there I'm certain you could look the world over and not find one as smart, organized, and as genuine as Ms. Eseed. Let me put it this way, how many lawyers do you actually look forward talking to? For myself, at this moment, I know of exactly one.

Everyone in the office, Mark, Carrie, Jennifer, Deanna, Elizabeth have helped turn a tragic event into something manageable and less painful. For that they have my everlasting gratitude.

Dean Zayed: On the investment management side, performance and responsiveness was reasonable over many years of service. reviews.text:
Back in early 2020 I started to search for a family law attorney to handle my divorce case that had recently been filed. I selected 5 attorneys that I wanted to interview and started the process to interview each of them. After speaking with all five I finally decided on Naveed Husain and his firm Farooqi & Husain. I made this choice for a few key reasons. First and foremost Naveed is a God fearing man. That told me that he would always be  honest with me. Secondly he was very direct and blunt with his communication with me. This directness and honesty is something I always look for in a lawyer and third Naveed understood my needs almost immediately. In our early meetings he very accurately predicted the outcome well before it materialized.

Once I selected him and his firm I got a chance to meet his staff. They were nothing short of the best legal support staff I have ever worked with.

From the very beginning Naveed and at times his partner Ausaf were incredibly strategic in how they planned my case. They had tremendous insight into how family law works and in practice how divorce proceedings would unfold as the case progressed. Naveed’s legal acumen never failed me once. Literally everything he said that would happened happened. In almost two years there was never a single instance where I was surprised. He told me where is was strong and where my case was weak. He was direct and honest as I expected and never once lead me to believe that I could and would gain any legal advantage that wasn’t realistic. Naveed understood the issues as a matter of law and navigated some very touchy issues with deadly accuracy and finesse. Where we needed to argue a matter before the judge Naveed prevailed every single time. In all my 24 years of being in business for myself and having dealt with over a dozen different lawyers with a variety of legal specializations Naveed and his legal mind impressed me the most.  He knew exactly what he was doing and nailed it 100% of the time. Never once did I ever feel like we could have done better. Not once.

Financially, working with Farooqi & Husain was an absolute blessing. Most lawyers bill in 15 to 30 minute increments. Which means that if you call them for 5 minutes they will charge you for 15 minutes or if you call them for 16 minutes they will charge you for 30 minutes. With Naveed his firm bills in 6 minute increments. Which to me as a client means that he is very fair when it comes to billing. No money wasted at all in unfair billing practices. His rates as a world class lawyer are incredibly fair. Even though, with his experience and acumen the could be billing at an exorbitant rate like most downtown Chicago attorneys would, Naveed maintained a just and very fair bill rate for himself and his staff. In the end I felt like I was working with a large downtown law firm with marble conference tables and a 55th floor view of the lake for prices that were more than reasonable. Never once, in the whole two years did I ever question a single invoice I received from his firm.

In the end, after an almost 2 year protracted battle that at times got ugly I got exactly what Naveed predicted I would get almost two years before when I met him in his office in Oakbrook Terrace.

Finally now, Alhamdolillah, my divorce is done. My ex got what she legally deserved and got what was legally fair to both her and the children and I got what I expected and was predicted. Naveed navigated some very tricky situations professionally and effectively.

All in all working with Naveed and his staff and firm was a fantastic experience and I will recommend him as much as I possibly can for anyone seeking assistance with any family law matter especially divorce cases.

Thank you Naveed for everything you did for me and thank you for your honesty, integrity and fairness. May Allah bless you InshAllah. Jazak Allah

Important advice for anyone hiring an attorney:

1. Verify attorney registration and insurance status via the ARDC Master Roll (public resource, updated annually)
2. Document all communications in writing from the outset (even if not sent to the attorney directly, send yourself contemporaneous emails noting what was discussed, with date and time)
3. Monitor your case via PACER (there may be small costs per docket, but it is worth knowing what is being represented to the court on your behalf)
4. Keep copies of everything
5. Advocate for yourself

These steps are especially important in employment and civil rights matters.

Alex Rothenberg served as the court-appointed GAL (Guardian ad Litem) in my case, and my experience with her was deeply troubling. Despite initially appearing engaged by asking questions and taking notes, she failed to address any of my concerns, displayed a clear bias toward my ex-wife, and accepted her statements as truth without requiring any supporting evidence.

Alex prematurely removed herself from the case, leaving critical issues unresolved and contributing to an outcome where my daughter has been denied fair and equal time with both parents. Her actions violated several key statutes and guidelines that GALs are required to follow in Illinois, including:

750 ILCS 5/506(a)(2): GALs must conduct an independent investigation, including interviews and a review of relevant documents, and provide an objective recommendation to the court. Alex failed to conduct a thorough investigation or verify evidence, instead relying on unchecked statements from my ex-wife.
Best Interest of the Child Standard (750 ILCS 5/602.7): GALs are required to prioritize the child’s well-being and ensure meaningful relationships with both parents unless there is clear evidence to the contrary. Alex’s biased and incomplete recommendations directly harmed my daughter’s ability to have a fair and balanced relationship with both parents.
Ethical Obligations of Attorneys and GALs: GALs are required to avoid personal bias, remain neutral, and act in a professional manner that serves the child’s best interests. Alex’s personal bias against me was evident and unprofessional, resulting in recommendations that prioritized her own prejudice over the needs of a 5-year-old child.
To make matters worse, Alex’s conduct was part of a broader pattern within her firm. Other members of her firm displayed similarly unethical behavior, including inflated billing practices, misrepresentations to the court, and a general failure to uphold professional standards.

Alex’s unprofessional and unethical behavior, combined with her failure to follow required statutes, caused irreparable harm to my daughter’s well-being. Her actions were not only a disservice to the court but also a direct violation of the trust placed in her to act in the best interests of the child.

Avoid Alex Rothenberg and her entire firm. Their lack of professionalism, integrity, and accountability makes them unfit for any legal matter, particularly those involving children.

She took money, produced no results and failed to deliver on promises she claimed were “based on facts.” An attorney should advocate for you with skill and compassion. Sharon did the opposite.

Her conduct was careless, her tone dismissive and her approach heartless. If you value integrity, empathy, and actual representation, look elsewhere. You deserve better.

reviews.text: My husband and I retained Melissa Kuffel concerning a medical related DCFS case.  My only regret is that I did not find her sooner.  DCFS is an incredibly difficult situation to navigate by yourself and the difference between a private attorney and a public defender is night and day.  I am certain that if we did not have her, we would still be in a frustrating legal mess.  Melissa is extremely knowledgeable about the DCFS agency and answered all of our questions thoughtfully.  She also constantly checked up on us to see how we were doing.  She is proactive rather than reactive and took all of our concerns seriously.  She is a realist and was not afraid to tell us things that we did not want to hear.  Her experience accurately predicted almost every situation we encountered throughout our case.  The fees were in the range that was estimated for us as well.  When we retained her, she told us she was confident she could get our lives back to normal within six months, and she delivered even with frustrating COVID delays.  We had a lot of luck on our side, but she was able to use that luck to our advantage.  I cannot recommend her enough.  If you are scared or frustrated with your DCFS case, you should definitely give her a call for a consultation.

reviews.text: I was represented by attorney Deanna Rosinski in this firm and was referred to her by one of my husband’s coworkers. When I met Deanna, I felt fully confident in her immediately. I went to her to in hope to settle a new child visitation schedule with my son’s father but there were so many complications that were thrown in the mix. Deanna was always accessible. She explained everything in layman’s terms and even made you feel calm when the stress of a seemingly seamless process turns into a bit of a nightmare. She predicted what would happen and was spot on, she was honest, transparent, fair and funny! She is down to earth and easy to talk to and I felt super confident in her capabilities and she was ALWAYS prepared and knew the details of my situation and the sensitivities involved when a mother must put her child first no matter how hard that can be! It’s hard not to be selfish when you love your kiddos so much and you want them at your side. Especially when there are questionable things going on at the other parent’s home and with their family.  I will go back to Deanna for future needs 100% 10/10 Recommend Deanna!
